era_context: Identical to -1630 and -1631: Late Bronze Age, 18th Dynasty Egypt, Hittite consolidation, Shang Dynasty China.

events.title: 18th Dynasty Egyptian imperial administration
events.description: The 18th Dynasty continues its imperial rule. This year falls within the reign of Thutmose III or his successor. The Egyptian state maintains its extensive administrative apparatus, military forces, and religious institutions. The construction of temples and monuments continues as a major state activity.

sources.name: Egyptian temple inscriptions and administrative records

events.title: Hittite state consolidation
events.description: The Hittite Empire continues its consolidation. The state is developing its characteristic administrative and military structures. Diplomatic relations with neighboring powers are maintained through correspondence and treaties.

sources.name: Hittite royal inscriptions and administrative texts

events.title: Shang Dynasty oracle bone practices
events.description: The Shang Dynasty continues its oracle bone divination practices. These bones record questions posed to ancestors and spirits regarding weather, harvests, military campaigns, and other matters of state concern.
events.certainty_note: Shang chronology remains debated; uncertainty ±50–100 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for -1632 distinct from -1630 and -1631.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -1632 is essentially identical to -1630 and -1631. Individual years in this period are archaeologically and textually indistinguishable; the significance of -1632 as a distinct historical moment is minimal.
